questions.text: What PactoSigna feature provides compiled evidence views (Risk Management File, V&V, DMR, DHR) for management review?

options.explanation: Quality Events manages complaints, NCs, and CAPAs — not compiled record views.
Per Section 5.4, PactoSigna's Records Page provides compiled views including Risk Management File, V&V, Design Master Record, and Device History Record.
The Training module manages training tasks, not compiled evidence records.
The sync engine imports documents from GitHub; it does not compile record views.

questions.text: What is the minimum frequency for scheduled management reviews?

options.explanation: Monthly exceeds the minimum requirement.
Quarterly exceeds the minimum requirement.
Per Section 5.1, management reviews shall be conducted at minimum annually, scheduled in Q1.
Biennial reviews do not meet the annual minimum requirement.

questions.text: A management review can be triggered outside the annual schedule by events such as major nonconformances or regulatory authority findings.
questions.explanation: Per Section 5.1, additional reviews shall be triggered by major nonconformances, regulatory findings, significant organizational changes, or product recalls.

questions.text: Where does the Quality Management Representative track management review action items in PactoSigna?

options.explanation: Management review actions are tracked in Quality Reviews, not the CAPA module.
Per Section 5.6, each action item has an assigned owner and target completion date, tracked in PactoSigna's Quality Reviews collection.
Action items are tracked within PactoSigna for auditability.
The Training module is for training tasks, not management review actions.

questions.text: How far in advance must the agenda and input data be distributed before a management review?

options.explanation: 1 day does not allow adequate preparation time.
Per Section 5.3, input data shall be distributed to attendees at least 5 business days before the review.
10 days exceeds the minimum requirement.
Advance distribution is required to allow attendees to prepare.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a required management review input?

options.text: CAPA status and effectiveness from PactoSigna's Quality Events module
options.explanation: CAPA status is a required input per Section 5.3.

options.text: Training compliance from PactoSigna's Training module
options.explanation: Training compliance is a required input per Section 5.3.
Individual performance ratings are not a management review input. The review focuses on QMS processes, not individual performance.

options.text: Quality objectives progress from PactoSigna's Quality Objectives entity
options.explanation: Quality objectives progress is a required input per Section 5.3.
